description: REQ-EPNS-009 (line 242) says EmptyNoteDiscarded.occurredOn is 'sourced from Clock.now() called by the flush step.' REQ-EPNS-012 (line 281) says 'EmptyNoteDiscarded.occurredOn is sourced from the same Clock.now() as startNewSession, OR from its own call.' The use of 'OR' makes the sourcing non-deterministic from the implementer's perspective: should flushCurrentSession call Clock.now() independently, or should it receive a pre-computed timestamp from a shared Clock.now() call? These two requirements give contradictory answers. REQ-EPNS-009 says flush step calls it; REQ-EPNS-012 says it might share the startNewSession call. Only one answer can be correct.

evidence.snippet: Line 242: 'EmptyNoteDiscarded.occurredOn is sourced from Clock.now() called by the flush step.' | Line 281: 'EmptyNoteDiscarded.occurredOn is sourced from the same Clock.now() as startNewSession, OR from its own call.'
